sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, value DOUBLE, date_value DATE);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, date_value) VALUES (1, 0.693, DATE '2023-01-01'), (2, 0.0, DATE '2023-01-02'), (3, -1.0, DATE '2023-01-03');
SELECT id, exp(value) AS exp_value, date_value - DATE '2023-01-01' AS date_diff FROM test_data WHERE exp(value) > 1.0;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, related_id INT, extra_value DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO another_table (id, related_id, extra_value) VALUES (1, 1, NULL), (2, 1, 2.718), (3, 2, 1.414);
SELECT a.id, exp(a.value) AS exp_value, a.date_value, (a.date_value - DATE '2023-01-01') AS date_diff FROM test_data a JOIN another_table b ON a.id = b.related_id WHERE b.extra_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, exp(value) AS exp_value FROM test_data WHERE exp(value) IS NULL OR value < -1.0;
WITH calculated_data AS (SELECT id, exp(value) AS exp_value, date_value - (SELECT MIN(date_value) FROM test_data) AS date_adjust FROM test_data) SELECT * FROM calculated_data HAVING exp_value > 1;
SELECT a.id, exp(a.value) - (SELECT exp(b.value) FROM test_data b WHERE b.id = 2) AS exp_diff FROM test_data a;
SELECT b.id, COUNT(a.id) AS record_count FROM test_data a JOIN another_table b ON a.id = b.related_id GROUP BY b.id HAVING COUNT(a.id) > 1;
SELECT exp(NULL) AS null_exp_value, NULLIF(exp(0), exp(0)) AS zero_difference;
